divergences.description: Agent B and Agent A classify community governance docs (CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) as CRITICAL (Week 1 fix). Agent C classifies them as IMPORTANT (post-release). The difference is whether contributor onboarding docs must be ready within a week or can wait longer. Both positions are defensible; the impact depends on how quickly external contributors arrive.

rationale: All three agents now agree on: the 19-issue scope, all 6 blockers (including CLAUDE.md operational identifiers — resolved in round 03 when Agent C elevated to BLOCKER), auth rate limiting as CRITICAL, the branding/lock-in/cosmetic framework, sanitize-not-delete for CLAUDE.md, remediation sequencing with exit criteria, and the release contract as the top strategic question. Agent A produced the cleanest convergence by fully adopting the consensus position. Agent C made the most significant analytical improvement this round by resolving the sole remaining round 02 disagreement (CLAUDE.md severity) and adding the best open questions (runbook publication controls, dependency scan ownership). The single remaining divergence — community docs severity — is minor and does not affect the remediation action itself, only its timing.
